[2018-11-14 20:45] zera777 (Zera): Actually I just wanted to make sure I got a good deal. For example, if I want the campaign and 2 puzzle packs, I could get the Engineer Tier and both packs for $45.
Or I could get the Grand Centurion Tier for $120. So for $75 more, I get
25 Power Cores & 14500 Shards
Emotes and avatars (it looks like they're not exclusive)
Two Platinum Tickets per week
Soundtrack & Art Book
That's 0% more gameplay, so it doesn't look like a good deal to me. I could buy 3-5 more games!
Anyways, if there are more puzzle packs in the future, especially if they are added to existing support tiers, that could change my decision.
I wonder how much the average paying player is supposed to pay. I don't know how expensive it is to run an online game like Prismata.

[2018-11-14 20:59] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): I bought the 25 dollar Engineer (I believe) pack, to play before it went free to play. I wanted to support the creator as well. This game is one of a kind, and has had a lot of heart put into it. And even though it's simple, the overall polish and feel of the interface and presentation is amazing. It's all crisp and sharp..the only real complaint I have is that it can be a bit static and boring at times..just a bunch of squares on the screen, ya know? But I have hopes they can dress it up more at some point. Units like Amporilla and Scorches have cool animations..so I think they could add more to the other units, especially the legendary ones. Lightning bolts sparking out all over the place as Thunderhead comes online. The screen shakes and cracks when Arka is purchased. A pulse of prismatic colors radiating out when Centrifuge pops. So on and so forth. As far as puzzle packs and stuff go..I'd buy the cheapest premium that includes it all currently. If more is added, either it will be included in that tier, or it's another opportunity to support the creator for worthwhile content.
